messages.content: How to download a private model from huggingface with the python code snapshot_download

# Downloading Private Models from Hugging Face

To download a private model using `snapshot_download`, you need to authenticate with your Hugging Face token. Here are the methods:

## Method 1: Using Token Parameter (Recommended)

```python
from huggingface_hub import snapshot_download

model_path = snapshot_download(
    repo_id="username/private-model-name",
    token="hf_YourAccessTokenHere"
)
```

## Method 2: Login First

```python
from huggingface_hub import snapshot_download, login

# Login once
login(token="hf_YourAccessTokenHere")

# Then download
model_path = snapshot_download(
    repo_id="username/private-model-name"
)
```

## Method 3: Using Environment Variable

```python
import os
from huggingface_hub import snapshot_download

# Set the token as an environment variable
os.environ["HF_TOKEN"] = "hf_YourAccessTokenHere"

# Or in your terminal before running:
# export HF_TOKEN="hf_YourAccessTokenHere"

model_path = snapshot_download(
    repo_id="username/private-model-name"
)
```

## Method 4: Using Hugging Face CLI

```bash
# Login via CLI (saves token for future use)
huggingface-cli login

# Then in Python, no token needed:
```

```python
from huggingface_hub import snapshot_download

model_path = snapshot_download(
    repo_id="username/private-model-name"
)
```

## Getting Your Access Token

1. Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens
2. Create a new token or copy an existing one
3. Make sure it has "read" permissions

## Additional Options

```python
model_path = snapshot_download(
    repo_id="username/private-model-name",
    token="hf_YourAccessTokenHere",
    cache_dir="/custom/cache/path",  # Optional: custom cache location
    revision="main",                  # Optional: specific branch/tag
    ignore_patterns=["*.msgpack"],   # Optional: skip certain files
)
```

The downloaded files will be stored in your Hugging Face cache directory by default (`~/.cache/huggingface/hub/`).
